name: Savory Indian Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 3/4-inch cube of peeled, fresh ginger root
For every one cup of soaked beans:
1/2 tsp cumin or other vegetable-seasoned salt
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak mung beans overnight to soften.
2. Drain the beans and prepare them for grinding by placing all ingredients in a food processor or blender. If using a food processor, process the entire batch; if using a blender, process one cup of beans at a time with the corresponding amount of other ingredients per batch.
3. Add the ginger root piece to the machine first, then add the soaked, drained mung beans and blend until the mixture is smooth and fluffy.
4. Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium flame and pour about 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of oil into the pan.
5. Mix the batter thoroughly with a spoon before each pancake pouring. Scoop out 1/3 to 1/2 cup of batter and place the rounded bottom of a broad spoon lightly in the center of the batter.
6. Spread the batter outward using a slow, gentle, and continuous spiral motion with the back of a soup spoon until the pancake is about 7-8 inches in diameter. If the batter sticks to the spoon and creates holes in the pancake, adjust your pressing technique or start spreading sooner.
7. Cover and cook for 2 minutes, or until the pancake turns reddish-brown. Remove the cover and drizzle a small amount of oil over the pancake.
8. Flip the pancake and cook on the other side uncovered until reddish spots begin to appear.
9. Cook all the batter in the same manner.
10. Serve plain with vegetable sides, chutneys, and yogurt, or stuff the pancakes with fillings and fold them into turnovers.
This recipe makes 18 to 20 pancakes.
